title: Relax non-functional tolerances on the steering knuckle machined faces
description: [ALL] Audit every toleranced dimension on the knuckle drawing against actual mating-part function; relax non-datum faces from IT7 to IT9-10 and widen positional tolerances on non-critical bolt bosses. Cuts CNC finishing passes, in-process gauging and CMM time; scrap

ideaData.title: Relax non-functional tolerances on the steering knuckle machined faces
ideaData.technicalDescription: Audit every toleranced dimension on the knuckle drawing against actual mating-part function; relax non-datum faces from IT7 to IT9-10 and widen positional tolerances on non-critical bolt bosses. Cuts CNC finishing passes, in-process gauging and CMM time; scrap falls with the wider window. Requires a tolerance-stack study to prove assembly function is preserved.
ideaData.materialGrade: 42CrMo4 / GJS-500 (unchanged) / Same material — spec change only / n/a
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Delete finishing pass on relaxed faces; Reduced CMM sampling plan
ideaData.riskNotes: Stack-up must be re-validated; supplier PPAP re-approval needed on the changed drawing.
processAlternatives: Delete finishing pass on relaxed faces

title: Widen caliper-bracket positional tolerances to match real fixture capability
description: [ALL] Positional tolerances on caliper-bracket holes are commonly specified at the machine-capability limit rather than the function limit. Widening from ±0.1 to ±0.25 where the bolted joint allows removes a dedicated drill-jig stage and lets the holes run in the ma

ideaData.title: Widen caliper-bracket positional tolerances to match real fixture capability
ideaData.technicalDescription: Positional tolerances on caliper-bracket holes are commonly specified at the machine-capability limit rather than the function limit. Widening from ±0.1 to ±0.25 where the bolted joint allows removes a dedicated drill-jig stage and lets the holes run in the main op.
ideaData.materialGrade: GJS-500 / 6082-T6 (unchanged)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Combine drilling into main machining op; Delete dedicated jig
ideaData.riskNotes: Brake-hose routing and pad wear clearance verified across the widened window.
processAlternatives: Combine drilling into main machining op

title: Replace tight gap-and-flush specs on hidden trim interfaces
description: [ALL] Gap/flush specs written for A-surfaces are frequently copied onto hidden interfaces (under-console, lower IP). Relaxing hidden-interface gaps from ±0.5 to ±1.5 mm removes mould re-cuts, slide actions and fitting rework at assembly.

ideaData.title: Replace tight gap-and-flush specs on hidden trim interfaces
ideaData.technicalDescription: Gap/flush specs written for A-surfaces are frequently copied onto hidden interfaces (under-console, lower IP). Relaxing hidden-interface gaps from ±0.5 to ±1.5 mm removes mould re-cuts, slide actions and fitting rework at assembly.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP-TD20 / ABS (unchanged)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Simpler mould without slide re-cuts; Reduced fitting rework
ideaData.riskNotes: Squeak & rattle validated at the wider gaps with foam/felt where needed.

title: Relax over-specified tolerances on the B-pillar reinforcement weld-flange
description: [ALL] Function-audit the B-pillar reinforcement weld-flange drawing: identify dimensions toleranced tighter than the mating interface requires (legacy copy-paste specs are endemic), relax to the function limit, and delete the finishing/gauging content the tight spec

ideaData.title: Relax over-specified tolerances on the B-pillar reinforcement weld-flange
ideaData.technicalDescription: Function-audit the B-pillar reinforcement weld-flange drawing: identify dimensions toleranced tighter than the mating interface requires (legacy copy-paste specs are endemic), relax to the function limit, and delete the finishing/gauging content the tight spec forced. Classic Boothroyd-Dewhurst tolerance-cost discipline: below IT8 the cost curve is exponential.
ideaData.materialGrade: Material unchanged — specification change only
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Delete finishing pass; Reduce CMM/gauging sampling
ideaData.riskNotes: Tolerance stack re-validated with the mating parts; PPAP delta approval on the B-pillar reinforcement weld-flange drawing.

title: Flow-drill screws replace welded nuts on the mixed-material rocker
description: [ALL] Where an Al rocker meets steel reinforcements, replace welded nuts + bolts with flow-drill screws (FDS): single-sided access, no pre-hole, joins Al-steel stacks without weld spatter or galvanic sleeves. Deletes nut-welding stations and hole-punching ops.

ideaData.title: Flow-drill screws replace welded nuts on the mixed-material rocker
ideaData.technicalDescription: Where an Al rocker meets steel reinforcements, replace welded nuts + bolts with flow-drill screws (FDS): single-sided access, no pre-hole, joins Al-steel stacks without weld spatter or galvanic sleeves. Deletes nut-welding stations and hole-punching ops.
ideaData.materialGrade: EN AW-6xxx + CR340 stacks / Structural adhesive companion bead
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Flow-drill screwing (FDS); Self-pierce riveting alternative
ideaData.riskNotes: FDS torque window and stripping validated per stack; service-release procedure for repairs.

title: Laser brazing replaces spot-weld + sealer on the roof-to-bodyside joint
description: [ALL] Convert the roof ditch joint from spot welds + finisher mouldings to laser brazing: a continuous sealed seam that deletes the ditch moulding, its clips and the sealer bead, and improves flushness.

ideaData.title: Laser brazing replaces spot-weld + sealer on the roof-to-bodyside joint
ideaData.technicalDescription: Convert the roof ditch joint from spot welds + finisher mouldings to laser brazing: a continuous sealed seam that deletes the ditch moulding, its clips and the sealer bead, and improves flushness.
ideaData.materialGrade: CuSi3 braze wire on galvanised steel
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Laser brazing cell; Roller hemming alternative
ideaData.riskNotes: Zinc outgassing porosity managed with wire/optics choice; A-surface braze quality gating.
materialAlternatives: CuSi3 braze wire on galvanised steel

title: Laser welding replaces bolted busbar joints inside the pack
description: [ALL] Replace bolted + torque-audited module busbar joints with laser-welded tabs: deletes fasteners, torque stations and re-torque audits, cuts contact resistance and heat. Weld depth control protects cells; vision-checked seams replace torque traceability.

ideaData.title: Laser welding replaces bolted busbar joints inside the pack
ideaData.technicalDescription: Replace bolted + torque-audited module busbar joints with laser-welded tabs: deletes fasteners, torque stations and re-torque audits, cuts contact resistance and heat. Weld depth control protects cells; vision-checked seams replace torque traceability.
ideaData.materialGrade: Al 1050/Cu hybrid busbars / Ni-plated Cu tabs
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Fiber-laser welding with beam wobble; Ultrasonic wedge bonding alternative
ideaData.riskNotes: Weld penetration windows validated against cell venting; serviceability strategy for module replacement.

title: Structural adhesive + SPR replaces bolts on the aluminium tailgate inner
description: [ALL] Structural adhesive + SPR replaces bolts on the aluminium tailgate inner. Modern joining substitutes the legacy fastened/welded interface: fewer parts, fewer stations, better sealed or lighter joints; validated by joint-strength, fatigue and service-repair stu

ideaData.title: Structural adhesive + SPR replaces bolts on the aluminium tailgate inner
ideaData.technicalDescription: Structural adhesive + SPR replaces bolts on the aluminium tailgate inner. Modern joining substitutes the legacy fastened/welded interface: fewer parts, fewer stations, better sealed or lighter joints; validated by joint-strength, fatigue and service-repair studies. Typical automotive adopters run this on current premium programmes.
ideaData.materialGrade: Joint materials unchanged; consumable/filler per process
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: See title process; Legacy joint as fallback
ideaData.riskNotes: Joint validation (static/fatigue/corrosion) and service-repair procedure required before cutover.
materialAlternatives: Joint materials unchanged; consumable/filler per process

title: Zonal harness consolidation deletes the door-module home-runs
description: [ALL] Move door electronics onto a zonal controller with a CAN-FD/LIN ring: the per-door home-run bundles collapse into short local stubs. Cuts copper mass, connector count and assembly routing time; the zonal ECU is amortised across four doors.

ideaData.title: Zonal harness consolidation deletes the door-module home-runs
ideaData.technicalDescription: Move door electronics onto a zonal controller with a CAN-FD/LIN ring: the per-door home-run bundles collapse into short local stubs. Cuts copper mass, connector count and assembly routing time; the zonal ECU is amortised across four doors.
ideaData.materialGrade: Recycled-copper harness strands / Al conductor for power runs
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Zonal E/E architecture; CAN-FD/LIN ring topology
ideaData.riskNotes: Zonal failure modes (single-point door loss) mitigated by limp strategies; EMC re-validation.

title: Consolidate seat/climate/door ECUs into one comfort domain controller
description: [ALL] Replace 4-6 single-function comfort ECUs with one domain controller running mixed-criticality software partitions. Deletes housings, connectors, and per-ECU flash/EOL stations; software becomes the integration point.

ideaData.title: Consolidate seat/climate/door ECUs into one comfort domain controller
ideaData.technicalDescription: Replace 4-6 single-function comfort ECUs with one domain controller running mixed-criticality software partitions. Deletes housings, connectors, and per-ECU flash/EOL stations; software becomes the integration point.
ideaData.materialGrade: n/a — electronics consolidation
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Domain-controller integration; AUTOSAR/hypervisor partitioning
ideaData.riskNotes: Supplier software integration risk; ASIL partitioning and OTA strategy must be owned in-house.

title: Replace shielded sensor lines with unshielded twisted pair + software filtering
description: [ALL] Several chassis/ADAS sensor runs carry legacy shielded cable specified before modern DSP filtering. Where EMC budgets allow, unshielded twisted pair with software-side filtering deletes the shield, drain wire, and shielded-connector premium.

ideaData.title: Replace shielded sensor lines with unshielded twisted pair + software filtering
ideaData.technicalDescription: Several chassis/ADAS sensor runs carry legacy shielded cable specified before modern DSP filtering. Where EMC budgets allow, unshielded twisted pair with software-side filtering deletes the shield, drain wire, and shielded-connector premium.
ideaData.materialGrade: UTP automotive cable / Recycled copper strands
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: DSP filtering at the receiver; Selective shielding only in proven-noisy zones
ideaData.riskNotes: EMC validation per run; keep shields on proven-critical paths (radar, audio).
processAlternatives: Selective shielding only in proven-noisy zones
